synopsis: Luther L. Allin, Jr., and other members of the Session of Westminster Presbyterian Church, Chattanooga, Tennessee, complained against Tennessee Valley Presbytery for improper handling of a complaint about the Session's refusal to call a congregational meeting. The Judicial Commission sustained errors regarding inadequate notice (less than 10 days), improper participation of Wallace Bristow and Pastor Vitullo as voting members with direct personal interest, and remanded the matter to Presbytery for proper rehearing.
